verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation and polybasic sequences. However, under cancer-associated conditions of dysregulated autophagy, SRC undergoes topological inversion via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), presenting the protein on the outer plasma membrane face with the catalytic domain extracellularly. This outer-leaflet presentation is via SRC's own mechanism (ALE-mediated secretory pathway), not transient non-covalent recruitment. This surface-inverted form (eSrc) can be targeted by antibody therapeutics including bispecific T cell engagers, ADCs, and radioligands, demonstrating functional surface accessibility under these defined conditions.
